settings.title: Fifteen Years of Olympic
settings.excerpt: This summer the gym turns fifteen, and some of our founding members still train here every week.
settings.body:
Olympic opened its doors fifteen years ago this summer, back when the building was half the size and the cardio kit was already out of date.

A lot has changed since, but the heart of the place has not. Several members who joined in our first year are still here, lifting alongside people who were not yet born when we opened.

How the place grew

The early years were lean. We added a rack when we could afford one, knocked through a wall when the membership outgrew the room, and slowly turned a tired building into a proper gym. Much of what you train on now was paid for one piece at a time, with the members who believed in the place from the start.

To mark the anniversary we are running a charity lift day in June, with members pledging total weight moved to raise money for the local food bank. Every kilo counts toward the total.

How the lift day works

You do not need to be strong to take part, just willing to add up what you move:

  • Train as normal on the day and log your total weight lifted.
  • Ask friends or family to pledge a small amount per hundred kilos.
  • Bring a tin for the food bank collection by the front desk.

If you have an old photograph of the gym from the early days, bring it in. We are putting together a wall of them for the summer, and the half-finished free weights corner of fifteen years ago is worth a laugh.

Thank you to everyone who has trained with us over the years. The community you built is the real achievement.
